overview: Boros Synthesizer is a midrange deck that generates massive card advantage by cycling cheap artifacts like Experimental Synthesizer with bounce creatures such as Glint Hawk and Kor Skyfisher. It establishes board control using flying threats, removal, and lifegain, while adapting its posture to play aggressively with Seeker of the Way against aggressive or control opponents.
win_conditions: Establish board presence with flying threats
Generate massive card advantage via artifact bounce loops
Apply aggressive pressure with Seeker of the Way
Outvalue opponents with removal and lifegain

keep_criteria: Hands with early artifacts like Experimental Synthesizer and bounce creatures
Hands featuring aggressive starts like Seeker of the Way
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early plays or interaction
Hands without a clear game plan for the matchup

examples.reason: Provides early value generation, board presence, and interaction, aligning with the deck's midrange posture.

early_game: Play cheap artifacts to draw cards
Cast bounce creatures to return artifacts to hand
Use removal on opponent's early threats
Optionally lead with Seeker of the Way for aggressive pressure
mid_game: Cycle artifacts repeatedly to generate card advantage
Establish flying threats to apply pressure
Manage resources carefully to avoid misplays
late_game: Outvalue opponents with continuous card draw
Use lifegain and removal to stabilize
Avoid letting games drag out against combo decks like Affinity

key_cards.name: Experimental Synthesizer
key_cards.notes: Costs only 1 red mana to draw a card, replacing the banned Prophetic Prism and enabling efficient cycling.

key_cards.name: Glint Hawk
key_cards.notes: Returns artifacts to hand when it enters the battlefield, enabling repeated casting and card draw.

key_cards.name: Kor Skyfisher
key_cards.notes: Works alongside Glint Hawk to bounce rocks and generate card advantage.

key_cards.name: Seeker of the Way
key_cards.notes: Can win games on its own and triggers for lifegain when rocks are played after it enters.

sequencing_tips: Play Seeker of the Way before casting draw rocks to trigger its lifegain ability
Wait for the optimal moment to destroy opponent's enchantments in GW Auras matchups
Prioritize aggressive plays over value generation against aggressive or control decks

matchups.advice: Use removal and flying creatures to answer their key threats. Post-side, Pyroblast and Electrickery improve the matchup. Make careful decisions to avoid falling behind.
Play as aggressively as possible and avoid letting the game drag out. Sideboard provides strong answers. Against Grixis, side in Pyroblast and out Experimental Synthesizer.

Adopt an aggressive posture to close out games quickly. Seeker of the Way is a powerful threat. Side in Leave no Trace if they run specific curses, and Relic of Progenitus against Black/Madness variants.
Wait for the right time to destroy enchantments and try to end the game quickly before they create huge creatures. Post-side improves the matchup significantly.

Mirror match requires experience, attention to detail, and careful mulligan/order of play decisions. Time management on MTGO can be crucial. Side in Relic of Progenitus only if opponent runs Omen of the Dead.

metadata.source: Cards Realm + Cards Realm + Cards Realm

overview_evidence.evidence_span: We can say that Boros Synthesizer is a midrange deck, which as I mentioned above, wants to start generating value with the rocks draws in the first turns, in addition to spending removals on the opponent's creatures and establishing a board with our flying threats.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: By doing this, it is possible to play them again and draw more cards and generate tons of card advantage
establishing a board with our flying threats

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: starting with Seeker of the Way and only then making playing your draw rocks to trigger its ability
It's important to wait for the right time to destroy the enemy's enchantments

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: opponent's main cards, which are Spellstutter Sprite and the ninjas, the Ninja of the Deep Hours and Moon-Circuit Hacker
Myr Enforcer and Frogmite are colorless. The combo with Disciple of the Vault
